title: Sending Email Messages

Sending Email Messages

Sending Email Messages

You can send MIME-compliant email messages by calling operations on the UPOPMAIL component.

  1. Instantiate the UPOPMAIL service using activate or newinstance. 
    ;in a ProcScript module, such as an operation, function, or Detail trigger
variables
 handle MailerHandle
endvariables
newinstance "UPOPMAIL", MailerHandle
  2. Logon to your SMTP server using the LogonSMTP operation. 
    ; Logon to the SMTP server
MailerHandle->LogonSMTP("mailhost.mygreatcorp.net", USER, PASSWORD)
  3. Specify the mail header information.

    At the very least, you need to set the From field and at least one of the recipient fields (To, CC, or BCC).

    If you want any message replies to be sent to an account other than the sender, specify the ReplyTo field.

    ; Specify the mail header information
MailerHandle->SetFrom(FROM.MESSAGE.DUMMY)
MailerHandle->SetReplyTo("noreply@mygreatcorp.net")
MailerHandle->SetToList(TO.MESSAGE.DUMMY)
MailerHandle->SetSubject(SUBJECT.MESSAGE.DUMMY)
MailerHandle->SetPriority("3")
  4. For an HTML message that includes inline images (or other objects), you need to ensure that each image link specifies a unique content ID, rather than a file path and name.

    You can use string manipulation functions to search and replace any such references in the HTML.

  5. Specify the message text and its MIME type using the SetMessage operation.

    You can specify several message parts, such as a plain text version and an HTML version. The recipient's client is responsible for deciding which version to display.

    You can only specify one message part per MIME type. If you use two setMessage with MIMEType=test/html, the second call will overwrite the first.

    Note: Uniface does not convert the plain text message to HTML, (or HTML to plain text), and it does not validate whether the data in the message actually matches the specified MIME type.
    ; Specify the message body
MailerHandle->SetMessage(TEXT.MESSAGE.DUMMY, "") ; plain text body
MailerHandle->SetMessage(HTML.MESSAGE.DUMMY, "MIMEType=text/html") ; HTML body
  6. For any inline images (or other objects), call the AddInlineFromFile operation.

    The reference file name must match the content ID referenced in the HTML message. If not MIME type is specified, the type that matches the file extension is used.

    mailApiHandle->AddInlineFromFile($INLINE1$, "MIMEType="image/jpg")
MailerHandle->AddInlineFromFile($INLINE1$, "")
  7. For each attachment, call the AddAttachmentFromFile operation. 
    MailerHandle->AddAttachmentFromFile(ATTACHMENT.MESSAGE.DUMMY, "")
  8. Send the email using the Send operation. 
    MailerHandle->Send("")
  9. While the email message is in memory, you can change any of the header, message, or attachment parts of the email. You can then send it again. 
    MailerHandle->SetToList ("archive@mygreatcorp.com")
MailerHandle->Send("")
  10. Clear the message from memory.

    The message is automatically cleared from memory when the UPOPMAIL instance is destroyed, but if you want to use the instance to send another message, you can explicitly clear it using the ClearMail operation.

    MailerHandle->ClearMail("")
  11. Logoff the SMTP server. 
    MailerHandle->LogoffSMTP("")
end; end SendMail function
